title: poa secunda var. stenophylla
intro: Poa secunda var. stenophylla, also known as Sand Bluegrass, is a perennial grass native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. Herbal preparations of Poa secunda var. stenophylla can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat colds, sore throats, and respiratory infections.
